attacking_style: Explosive accelerations in final 200-500m on super-steep gradients (12%+), particularly effective at advanced age when races come down to short, punchy finishes rather than sustained climbing efforts.
team_tactics: Movistar sets tempo through domestiques (Quintana, Soler, Mas) before Valverde's late attacks, though team hierarchy confusion sometimes burns him on early pace-setting duties, leaving him unsupported in critical moments.
terrain_preference: Super-steep pitches (12%+) in final kilometers and attritional one-day races. Excels in reduced bunch sprints and late-race selections. Struggles on sustained altitude climbs and explosive accelerations beyond 3km to go.
vulnerability: Age-related decline in repeated accelerations over 3km, wind awareness/positioning in echelon splits, technical descents (crash history), and sustained efforts at altitude. Cannot match young explosive climbers on longer efforts.
key_patterns: Most dangerous within final 500m on super-steep ramps, multiple podiums from late acceleration (3 observations)
Still competitive in reduced sprints at 41+ years, securing podiums from breakaway remnants (3 observations)
Vulnerable to echelon splits and positioning errors, loses 20-30s in wind/technical stages (2 observations)
Cannot sustain repeated accelerations beyond 3km, drops when young climbers attack early (4 observations)
Team confusion wastes his efforts on domestique duties or leaves him chasing alone (3 observations)
Performs best in attritional one-day races rather than three-week Grand Tour efforts (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: Clear decline from 2019-2022, dropped on non-selective terrain (2022 Giro), illness-affected performances, though still capable of occasional podiums in suited one-day races (Strade Bianche 2nd at 41)
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 7
